source.title: Florida's 2024 Hurricane Season Cost Billions in Claims, and Reece Windows & Doors Sees Surge in Demand for Impact Windows Across Jacksonville, Tampa, and Orlando - markets.businessinsider.com
source.content:
Jacksonville, Florida, United States, June 6, 2026 -- Florida's 2024 hurricane season was one of the most active on record. Storms Debby, Helene, and Milton all made landfall within months of each other. As per reports on Earth.Org, hurricanes Milton and Helene led to $60 billion and $55 billion in damage, respectively. The financial impact on Florida homeowners was severe. Insurance premiums rose, and deductibles increased. Many homeowners discovered their coverage fell short when they needed it most. For many families, the storms exposed just how financially vulnerable homeownership can become in the face of increasingly severe weather events. [...] Insurance costs remain high. However, homeowners who upgrade to certified impact windows can qualify for meaningful premium reductions. That financial pressure is driving action. Florida homeowners in Jacksonville are accelerating plans to upgrade aging windows and doors before the next storm season. The calculation is straightforward for many. Property owners know the cost of a window upgrade. They can risk abandoning upgrades since the cost of storm damage or a denied insurance claim can be very high. Upgrading before the season begins is the only way to qualify for coverage and wind-mitigation discounts before a claim is needed. [...] My Markets Watchlist

Markets   News

# Florida's 2024 Hurricane Season Cost Billions in Claims, and Reece Windows & Doors Sees Surge in Demand for Impact Windows Across Jacksonville, Tampa, and Orlando

PRESS RELEASE Plentisoft

Jun. 5, 2026, 08:23 PM

Florida's 2024 hurricane season left homeowners facing higher premiums, tighter coverage, and a clearer understanding of storm risk. In response, demand for impact-resistant window upgrades is growing across Florida, and Reece Windows & Doors is positioned to meet it.

# Self-defense experts encourage women to learn basic safety skills

As concerns over attacks on metro Atlanta women grow, self-defense instructors emphasize awareness.

Credit: WXIA

MARIETTA, Ga. — Recent deadly attacks against women on the Beltline and a MARTA train have renewed conversations about personal safety, prompting self-defense experts to encourage women to learn practical skills that can help them recognize danger and respond effectively in threatening situations. [...] At Tommy Cho Martial Arts in Marietta, the focus is on more than physical techniques. Instructors say that building confidence and awareness can help women better protect themselves in potentially dangerous encounters.

This story continues below.

More in News

 DeKalb County investigating report of shooting
 Popular Atlanta club owner shot and killed over holiday weekend in Gwinnett County

"Equipping women with the confidence to get out of a dangerous situation is the main goal of self-defense," said Raquel Riley Thomas, founder of DefendRILEY.org and a black belt martial artist.

For Thomas, teaching self-defense is deeply personal. She says a family tragedy shaped her mission. [...] "Back in March of 2013, my mother took her life, and I found out later that she had been sexually assaulted when she was younger," Thomas said.

After moving to Georgia several years ago, Thomas became increasingly concerned about reports of assaults involving women. She said her martial arts training helped her discover a greater purpose.

"When I got here and started to take Taekwondo, I knew there was a purpose for me that was bigger than me," she said.

Today, Thomas works alongside black belt instructor David Ellard to teach Gracie Jiu-Jitsu to women and children of all ages. Their classes focus on practical self-defense techniques, situational awareness, and strategies for escaping dangerous situations.

# Murder charge dropped for groom who killed bride’s stepfather at wedding

By WSBTV.com News Staff

By WSBTV.com News Staff

BUTTS COUNTY, Ga. — Charges have been dropped against a man who said he fatally shot his wife’s stepfather on his wedding night in self-defense.

In a Channel 2 exclusive, Aaron White says in July 2024 he was a groom who just hours after his wedding took a step he replays every day.

He told Channel 2 Investigative Reporter Mark Winne shot his new stepfather in law, Jason Maughon, in self-defense in Butts County.

“Sorry somebody had to lose their life, but I had to do what I had to do to protect,” White said.

He was indicted earlier this year for murder and aggravated assault charges. [...] This order bearing the names of Flint Circuit District Attorney Darius Patillo and Judge Thomas Wilson says, “After a thorough review of the evidence, witness statements, investigative materials, and applicable law, the State has determined that it cannot meet its burden of proof beyond a reasonable doubt at trial due to insufficient evidence.”

They determined it cannot be proved beyond a reasonable doubt that White was the main aggressor.

Defense Attorney Bret Dunn, who said he and John Strauss represent White, says while Patillo is the Henry County D.A. he was appointed to handle the case after the D.A’s office that covers Butts County agreed to be disqualified from the case.

# Suspect sought in MARTA train shooting; law enforcement presence to increase

By WSBTV.com News Staff

By WSBTV.com News Staff

ATLANTA — Police with Metro Atlanta Regional Transportation Authority provided additional details in connection to a shooting they say was targeted.

Suspect sought in MARTA train shooting. What authorities say happened just before, LIVE on WSB Tonight at 11 p.m.

Channel 2 Action News reported the shooting happened at Midtown Station Friday night.

According to a preliminary police investigation, both men paid their fare to enter Midtown Station and, at first, got into separate train cars.

Gilchrist said the monitoring stations should be up and running within the next few weeks.

### Insurance Jobs [...] After local firefighters subdued the vehicle blaze on May 22, the state Fire Marshal investigators determined the fire had been deliberately set, Commissioner John King’s office said in a bulletin. The gasoline and the battery had been removed from the vehicle before the incident.

It may be the latest arson in the same area in the last eight months, including a camper fire, an outbuilding and non-residential structure fire, the office noted.

“We are asking for the public’s help to identify whoever is responsible for this fire,” King said in a statement. “A reward of up to $10,000 is available for information leading to the arrest and conviction of those involved. Callers may remain anonymous.” [...] Skip to content

# Jacob Misiorowski throws 103.7 mph pitch, fastest by a starter since tracking began in 2008

By Associated Press

By Associated Press

DENVER — Milwaukee Brewers ace Jacob Misiorowski threw a 103.7 mph pitch Saturday night against the Colorado Rockies, the fastest by a starter since tracking began in 2008.

The 24-year-old right-hander's record-setting pitch was low and outside to Kyle Karros in the third inning.

Misiorowski has thrown the 12 fastest pitches by a starter. His previous high was 103.4 mph against St. Louis on May 25.
